props.name: open
props.description:
Indicates whether or not the alert is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the alert, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the alert's open state.

props.name: closable
props.description: Enables a close button that allows the user to dismiss the alert.

props.name: variant
props.description: The alert's theme variant.

props.name: duration
props.description:
The length of time, in milliseconds, the alert will show before closing itself. If the user interacts with
the alert before it closes (e.g. moves the mouse over it), the timer will restart. Defaults to `Infinity`, meaning
the alert will not close on its own.

props.name: countdown
props.description:
Enables a countdown that indicates the remaining time the alert will be displayed.
Typically used to indicate the remaining time before a whole app refresh.

props.name: onShow
props.description: Emitted when the alert opens. (CEM event "sl-show")

props.name: onAfterShow
props.description: Emitted after the alert opens and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-show")

props.name: onHide
props.description: Emitted when the alert closes. (CEM event "sl-hide")

props.name: onAfterHide
props.description: Emitted after the alert closes and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-hide")

props.name: src
props.description: The path to the image to load.

props.name: alt
props.description: A description of the image used by assistive devices.

props.name: play
props.description: Plays the animation. When this attribute is remove, the animation will pause.

props.name: onLoad
props.description: Emitted when the image loads successfully. (CEM event "sl-load")

props.name: onError
props.description: Emitted when the image fails to load. (CEM event "sl-error")

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the built-in animation to use. For custom animations, use the `keyframes` prop.

props.name: play
props.description:
Plays the animation. When omitted, the animation will be paused. This attribute will be automatically removed when
the animation finishes or gets canceled.

props.name: delay
props.description: The number of milliseconds to delay the start of the animation.

props.name: direction
props.description:
Determines the direction of playback as well as the behavior when reaching the end of an iteration.
[Learn more](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/animation-direction)

props.name: duration
props.description: The number of milliseconds each iteration of the animation takes to complete.

props.name: easing
props.description:
The easing function to use for the animation. This can be a Shoelace easing function or a custom easing function
such as `cubic-bezier(0, 1, .76, 1.14)`.

props.name: end-delay
props.description: The number of milliseconds to delay after the active period of an animation sequence.

props.name: fill
props.description: Sets how the animation applies styles to its target before and after its execution.

props.name: iterations
props.description: The number of iterations to run before the animation completes. Defaults to `Infinity`, which loops.

props.name: iteration-start
props.description: The offset at which to start the animation, usually between 0 (start) and 1 (end).

props.name: playback-rate
props.description:
Sets the animation's playback rate. The default is `1`, which plays the animation at a normal speed. Setting this
to `2`, for example, will double the animation's speed. A negative value can be used to reverse the animation. This
value can be changed without causing the animation to restart.

props.name: endDelay
props.description: The number of milliseconds to delay after the active period of an animation sequence.

props.name: iterationStart
props.description: The offset at which to start the animation, usually between 0 (start) and 1 (end).

props.name: keyframes
props.description: The keyframes to use for the animation. If this is set, `name` will be ignored.

props.name: playbackRate
props.description:
Sets the animation's playback rate. The default is `1`, which plays the animation at a normal speed. Setting this
to `2`, for example, will double the animation's speed. A negative value can be used to reverse the animation. This
value can be changed without causing the animation to restart.

props.name: currentTime
props.description: Gets and sets the current animation time.

props.name: onCancel
props.description: Emitted when the animation is canceled. (CEM event "sl-cancel")

props.name: onFinish
props.description: Emitted when the animation finishes. (CEM event "sl-finish")

props.name: onStart
props.description: Emitted when the animation starts or restarts. (CEM event "sl-start")

props.name: image
props.description: The image source to use for the avatar.

props.name: label
props.description: A label to use to describe the avatar to assistive devices.

props.name: initials
props.description: Initials to use as a fallback when no image is available (1-2 characters max recommended).

props.name: loading
props.description: Indicates how the browser should load the image.

props.name: shape
props.description: The shape of the avatar.

props.name: onError
props.description: The image could not be loaded. This may because of an invalid URL, a temporary network condition, or some unknown cause. (CEM event "sl-error")

props.name: variant
props.description: The badge's theme variant.

props.name: pill
props.description: Draws a pill-style badge with rounded edges.

props.name: pulse
props.description: Makes the badge pulsate to draw attention.

props.name: label
props.description:
The label to use for the breadcrumb control. This will not be shown on the screen, but it will be announced by
screen readers and other assistive devices to provide more context for users.

props.name: href
props.description:
Optional URL to direct the user to when the breadcrumb item is activated. When set, a link will be rendered
internally. When unset, a button will be rendered instead.

props.name: target
props.description: Tells the browser where to open the link. Only used when `href` is set.

props.name: rel
props.description: The `rel` attribute to use on the link. Only used when `href` is set.

props.name: variant
props.description: The button's theme variant.

props.name: size
props.description: The button's size.

props.name: caret
props.description: Draws the button with a caret. Used to indicate that the button triggers a dropdown menu or similar behavior.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the button.

props.name: loading
props.description: Draws the button in a loading state.

props.name: outline
props.description: Draws an outlined button.

props.name: pill
props.description: Draws a pill-style button with rounded edges.

props.name: circle
props.description:
Draws a circular icon button. When this attribute is present, the button expects a single `<sl-icon>` in the
default slot.

props.name: type
props.description:
The type of button. Note that the default value is `button` instead of `submit`, which is opposite of how native
`<button>` elements behave. When the type is `submit`, the button will submit the surrounding form.

props.name: name
props.description:
The name of the button, submitted as a name/value pair with form data, but only when this button is the submitter.
This attribute is ignored when `href` is present.

props.name: value
props.description:
The value of the button, submitted as a pair with the button's name as part of the form data, but only when this
button is the submitter. This attribute is ignored when `href` is present.

props.name: href
props.description: When set, the underlying button will be rendered as an `<a>` with this `href` instead of a `<button>`.

props.name: target
props.description: Tells the browser where to open the link. Only used when `href` is present.

props.name: rel
props.description:
When using `href`, this attribute will map to the underlying link's `rel` attribute. Unlike regular links, the
default is `noreferrer noopener` to prevent security exploits. However, if you're using `target` to point to a
specific tab/window, this will prevent that from working correctly. You can remove or change the default value by
setting the attribute to an empty string or a value of your choice, respectively.

props.name: download
props.description: Tells the browser to download the linked file as this filename. Only used when `href` is present.

props.name: form
props.description:
The "form owner" to associate the button with. If omitted, the closest containing form will be used instead. The
value of this attribute must be an id of a form in the same document or shadow root as the button.

props.name: formaction
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `action` attribute.

props.name: formenctype
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `enctype` attribute.

props.name: formmethod
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `method` attribute.

props.name: formnovalidate
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `novalidate` attribute.

props.name: formtarget
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `target` attribute.

props.name: formAction
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `action` attribute.

props.name: formEnctype
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `enctype` attribute.

props.name: formMethod
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `method` attribute.

props.name: formNoValidate
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `novalidate` attribute.

props.name: formTarget
props.description: Used to override the form owner's `target` attribute.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the button loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the button gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: label
props.description:
A label to use for the button group. This won't be displayed on the screen, but it will be announced by assistive
devices when interacting with the control and is strongly recommended.

props.name: loop
props.description: When set, allows the user to navigate the carousel in the same direction indefinitely.

props.name: navigation
props.description: When set, show the carousel's navigation.

props.name: pagination
props.description: When set, show the carousel's pagination indicators.

props.name: autoplay
props.description: When set, the slides will scroll automatically when the user is not interacting with them.

props.name: autoplay-interval
props.description: Specifies the amount of time, in milliseconds, between each automatic scroll.

props.name: slides-per-page
props.description: Specifies how many slides should be shown at a given time.

props.name: slides-per-move
props.description:
Specifies the number of slides the carousel will advance when scrolling, useful when specifying a `slides-per-page`
greater than one. It can't be higher than `slides-per-page`.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Specifies the orientation in which the carousel will lay out.

props.name: mouse-dragging
props.description: When set, it is possible to scroll through the slides by dragging them with the mouse.

props.name: autoplayInterval
props.description: Specifies the amount of time, in milliseconds, between each automatic scroll.

props.name: slidesPerPage
props.description: Specifies how many slides should be shown at a given time.

props.name: slidesPerMove
props.description:
Specifies the number of slides the carousel will advance when scrolling, useful when specifying a `slides-per-page`
greater than one. It can't be higher than `slides-per-page`.

props.name: mouseDragging
props.description: When set, it is possible to scroll through the slides by dragging them with the mouse.

props.name: onSlideChange
props.description: Emitted when the active slide changes. (CEM event "sl-slide-change")

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: value
props.description: The current value of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: size
props.description: The checkbox's size.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the checkbox.

props.name: checked
props.description: Draws the checkbox in a checked state.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description:
Draws the checkbox in an indeterminate state. This is usually applied to checkboxes that represents a "select
all/none" behavior when associated checkboxes have a mix of checked and unchecked states.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: required
props.description: Makes the checkbox a required field.

props.name: help-text
props.description: The checkbox's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: defaultChecked
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: helpText
props.description: The checkbox's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the checkbox loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when the checked state changes. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the checkbox gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the checkbox receives input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: value
props.description:
The current value of the color picker. The value's format will vary based the `format` attribute. To get the value
in a specific format, use the `getFormattedValue()` method. The value is submitted as a name/value pair with form
data.

props.name: label
props.description:
The color picker's label. This will not be displayed, but it will be announced by assistive devices. If you need to
display HTML, you can use the `label` slot` instead.

props.name: format
props.description:
The format to use. If opacity is enabled, these will translate to HEXA, RGBA, HSLA, and HSVA respectively. The color
picker will accept user input in any format (including CSS color names) and convert it to the desired format.

props.name: inline
props.description: Renders the color picker inline rather than in a dropdown.

props.name: size
props.description: Determines the size of the color picker's trigger. This has no effect on inline color pickers.

props.name: no-format-toggle
props.description: Removes the button that lets users toggle between format.

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the form control, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the color picker.

props.name: hoist
props.description:
Enable this option to prevent the panel from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all, scenarios.

props.name: opacity
props.description: Shows the opacity slider. Enabling this will cause the formatted value to be HEXA, RGBA, or HSLA.

props.name: uppercase
props.description: By default, values are lowercase. With this attribute, values will be uppercase instead.

props.name: swatches
props.description:
One or more predefined color swatches to display as presets in the color picker. Can include any format the color
picker can parse, including HEX(A), RGB(A), HSL(A), HSV(A), and CSS color names. Each color must be separated by a
semicolon (`;`). Alternatively, you can pass an array of color values to this property using JavaScript.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: required
props.description: Makes the color picker a required field.

props.name: dependencies
props.default: { 'sl-button-group': SlButtonGroup, 'sl-button': SlButton, 'sl-dropdown': SlDropdown, 'sl-icon': SlIcon, 'sl-input': SlInput, 'sl-visually-hidden': SlVisuallyHidden }

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: noFormatToggle
props.description: Removes the button that lets users toggle between format.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the color picker loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when the color picker's value changes. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the color picker receives focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the color picker receives input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: value
props.description: The text value to copy.

props.name: from
props.description:
An id that references an element in the same document from which data will be copied. If both this and `value` are
present, this value will take precedence. By default, the target element's `textContent` will be copied. To copy an
attribute, append the attribute name wrapped in square brackets, e.g. `from="el[value]"`. To copy a property,
append a dot and the property name, e.g. `from="el.value"`.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the copy button.

props.name: copy-label
props.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip.

props.name: success-label
props.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip after copying.

props.name: error-label
props.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip when a copy error occurs.

props.name: feedback-duration
props.description: The length of time to show feedback before restoring the default trigger.

props.name: tooltip-placement
props.description: The preferred placement of the tooltip.

props.name: hoist
props.description:
Enable this option to prevent the tooltip from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|hidden|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all,
scenarios.

props.name: copyLabel
props.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip.

props.name: successLabel
props.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip after copying.

props.name: errorLabel
props.description: A custom label to show in the tooltip when a copy error occurs.

props.name: feedbackDuration
props.description: The length of time to show feedback before restoring the default trigger.

props.name: tooltipPlacement
props.description: The preferred placement of the tooltip.

props.name: onCopy
props.description: Emitted when the data has been copied. (CEM event "sl-copy")

props.name: onError
props.description: Emitted when the data could not be copied. (CEM event "sl-error")

props.name: open
props.description:
Indicates whether or not the details is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the details, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the details' open state.

props.name: summary
props.description: The summary to show in the header. If you need to display HTML, use the `summary` slot instead.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the details so it can't be toggled.

props.name: onShow
props.description: Emitted when the details opens. (CEM event "sl-show")

props.name: onAfterShow
props.description: Emitted after the details opens and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-show")

props.name: onHide
props.description: Emitted when the details closes. (CEM event "sl-hide")

props.name: onAfterHide
props.description: Emitted after the details closes and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-hide")

props.name: open
props.description:
Indicates whether or not the dialog is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the dialog, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the dialog's open state.

props.name: label
props.description:
The dialog's label as displayed in the header. You should always include a relevant label even when using
`no-header`, as it is required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

props.name: no-header
props.description:
Disables the header. This will also remove the default close button, so please ensure you provide an easy,
accessible way for users to dismiss the dialog.

props.name: modal
props.description: Exposes the internal modal utility that controls focus trapping. To temporarily disable focus trapping and allow third-party modals spawned from an active Shoelace modal, call `modal.activateExternal()` when the third-party modal opens. Upon closing, call `modal.deactivateExternal()` to restore Shoelace's focus trapping.

props.name: noHeader
props.description:
Disables the header. This will also remove the default close button, so please ensure you provide an easy,
accessible way for users to dismiss the dialog.

props.name: onShow
props.description: Emitted when the dialog opens. (CEM event "sl-show")

props.name: onAfterShow
props.description: Emitted after the dialog opens and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-show")

props.name: onHide
props.description: Emitted when the dialog closes. (CEM event "sl-hide")

props.name: onAfterHide
props.description: Emitted after the dialog closes and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-hide")

props.name: onInitialFocus
props.description: Emitted when the dialog opens and is ready to receive focus. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will prevent focusing and allow you to set it on a different element, such as an input. (CEM event "sl-initial-focus")

props.name: onRequestClose
props.description: Emitted when the user attempts to close the dialog by clicking the close button, clicking the overlay, or pressing escape. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will keep the dialog open. Avoid using this unless closing the dialog will result in destructive behavior such as data loss. (CEM event "sl-request-close")

props.name: vertical
props.description: Draws the divider in a vertical orientation.

props.name: open
props.description:
Indicates whether or not the drawer is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the drawer, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the drawer's open state.

props.name: label
props.description:
The drawer's label as displayed in the header. You should always include a relevant label even when using
`no-header`, as it is required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

props.name: placement
props.description: The direction from which the drawer will open.

props.name: contained
props.description:
By default, the drawer slides out of its containing block (usually the viewport). To make the drawer slide out of
its parent element, set this attribute and add `position: relative` to the parent.

props.name: no-header
props.description:
Removes the header. This will also remove the default close button, so please ensure you provide an easy,
accessible way for users to dismiss the drawer.

props.name: modal
props.description: Exposes the internal modal utility that controls focus trapping. To temporarily disable focus trapping and allow third-party modals spawned from an active Shoelace modal, call `modal.activateExternal()` when the third-party modal opens. Upon closing, call `modal.deactivateExternal()` to restore Shoelace's focus trapping.

props.name: noHeader
props.description:
Removes the header. This will also remove the default close button, so please ensure you provide an easy,
accessible way for users to dismiss the drawer.

props.name: onShow
props.description: Emitted when the drawer opens. (CEM event "sl-show")

props.name: onAfterShow
props.description: Emitted after the drawer opens and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-show")

props.name: onHide
props.description: Emitted when the drawer closes. (CEM event "sl-hide")

props.name: onAfterHide
props.description: Emitted after the drawer closes and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-hide")

props.name: onInitialFocus
props.description: Emitted when the drawer opens and is ready to receive focus. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will prevent focusing and allow you to set it on a different element, such as an input. (CEM event "sl-initial-focus")

props.name: onRequestClose
props.description: Emitted when the user attempts to close the drawer by clicking the close button, clicking the overlay, or pressing escape. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will keep the drawer open. Avoid using this unless closing the drawer will result in destructive behavior such as data loss. (CEM event "sl-request-close")

props.name: open
props.description:
Indicates whether or not the dropdown is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the dropdown, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the dropdown's open state.

props.name: placement
props.description:
The preferred placement of the dropdown panel. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the panel
inside of the viewport.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the dropdown so the panel will not open.

props.name: stay-open-on-select
props.description:
By default, the dropdown is closed when an item is selected. This attribute will keep it open instead. Useful for
dropdowns that allow for multiple interactions.

props.name: distance
props.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel away from its trigger.

props.name: skidding
props.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel along its trigger.

props.name: hoist
props.description:
Enable this option to prevent the panel from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all, scenarios.

props.name: sync
props.description: Syncs the popup width or height to that of the trigger element.

props.name: stayOpenOnSelect
props.description:
By default, the dropdown is closed when an item is selected. This attribute will keep it open instead. Useful for
dropdowns that allow for multiple interactions.

props.name: containingElement
props.description:
The dropdown will close when the user interacts outside of this element (e.g. clicking). Useful for composing other
components that use a dropdown internally.

props.name: onShow
props.description: Emitted when the dropdown opens. (CEM event "sl-show")

props.name: onAfterShow
props.description: Emitted after the dropdown opens and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-show")

props.name: onHide
props.description: Emitted when the dropdown closes. (CEM event "sl-hide")

props.name: onAfterHide
props.description: Emitted after the dropdown closes and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-hide")

props.name: value
props.description: The number to format in bytes.

props.name: unit
props.description: The type of unit to display.

props.name: display
props.description: Determines how to display the result, e.g. "100 bytes", "100 b", or "100b".

props.name: date
props.description:
The date/time to format. If not set, the current date and time will be used. When passing a string, it's strongly
recommended to use the ISO 8601 format to ensure timezones are handled correctly. To convert a date to this format
in JavaScript, use [`date.toISOString()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toISOString).

props.name: weekday
props.description: The format for displaying the weekday.

props.name: era
props.description: The format for displaying the era.

props.name: year
props.description: The format for displaying the year.

props.name: month
props.description: The format for displaying the month.

props.name: day
props.description: The format for displaying the day.

props.name: hour
props.description: The format for displaying the hour.

props.name: minute
props.description: The format for displaying the minute.

props.name: second
props.description: The format for displaying the second.

props.name: time-zone-name
props.description: The format for displaying the time.

props.name: time-zone
props.description: The time zone to express the time in.

props.name: hour-format
props.description: The format for displaying the hour.

props.name: timeZoneName
props.description: The format for displaying the time.

props.name: timeZone
props.description: The time zone to express the time in.

props.name: hourFormat
props.description: The format for displaying the hour.

props.name: value
props.description: The number to format.

props.name: type
props.description: The formatting style to use.

props.name: no-grouping
props.description: Turns off grouping separators.

props.name: currency
props.description: The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) currency code to use when formatting.

props.name: currency-display
props.description: How to display the currency.

props.name: minimum-integer-digits
props.description: The minimum number of integer digits to use. Possible values are 1-21.

props.name: minimum-fraction-digits
props.description: The minimum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are 0-20.

props.name: maximum-fraction-digits
props.description: The maximum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are 0-0.

props.name: minimum-significant-digits
props.description: The minimum number of significant digits to use. Possible values are 1-21.

props.name: maximum-significant-digits
props.description: The maximum number of significant digits to use,. Possible values are 1-21.

props.name: noGrouping
props.description: Turns off grouping separators.

props.name: currencyDisplay
props.description: How to display the currency.

props.name: minimumIntegerDigits
props.description: The minimum number of integer digits to use. Possible values are 1-21.

props.name: minimumFractionDigits
props.description: The minimum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are 0-20.

props.name: maximumFractionDigits
props.description: The maximum number of fraction digits to use. Possible values are 0-0.

props.name: minimumSignificantDigits
props.description: The minimum number of significant digits to use. Possible values are 1-21.

props.name: maximumSignificantDigits
props.description: The maximum number of significant digits to use,. Possible values are 1-21.

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the icon to draw. Available names depend on the icon library being used.

props.name: src
props.description:
An external URL of an SVG file. Be sure you trust the content you are including, as it will be executed as code and
can result in XSS attacks.

props.name: label
props.description:
An alternate description to use for assistive devices. If omitted, the icon will be considered presentational and
ignored by assistive devices.

props.name: library
props.description: The name of a registered custom icon library.

props.name: onLoad
props.description: Emitted when the icon has loaded. When using `spriteSheet: true` this will not emit. (CEM event "sl-load")

props.name: onError
props.description: Emitted when the icon fails to load due to an error. When using `spriteSheet: true` this will not emit. (CEM event "sl-error")

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the icon to draw. Available names depend on the icon library being used.

props.name: library
props.description: The name of a registered custom icon library.

props.name: src
props.description:
An external URL of an SVG file. Be sure you trust the content you are including, as it will be executed as code and
can result in XSS attacks.

props.name: href
props.description: When set, the underlying button will be rendered as an `<a>` with this `href` instead of a `<button>`.

props.name: target
props.description: Tells the browser where to open the link. Only used when `href` is set.

props.name: download
props.description: Tells the browser to download the linked file as this filename. Only used when `href` is set.

props.name: label
props.description:
A description that gets read by assistive devices. For optimal accessibility, you should always include a label
that describes what the icon button does.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the button.

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the icon button loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the icon button gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: position
props.description: The position of the divider as a percentage.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when the position changes. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: src
props.description:
The location of the HTML file to include. Be sure you trust the content you are including as it will be executed as
code and can result in XSS attacks.

props.name: mode
props.description: The fetch mode to use.

props.name: allow-scripts
props.description:
Allows included scripts to be executed. Be sure you trust the content you are including as it will be executed as
code and can result in XSS attacks.

props.name: allowScripts
props.description:
Allows included scripts to be executed. Be sure you trust the content you are including as it will be executed as
code and can result in XSS attacks.

props.name: onLoad
props.description: Emitted when the included file is loaded. (CEM event "sl-load")

props.name: onError
props.description: Emitted when the included file fails to load due to an error. (CEM event "sl-error")

props.name: type
props.description:
The type of input. Works the same as a native `<input>` element, but only a subset of types are supported. Defaults
to `text`.

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the input, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: value
props.description: The current value of the input, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: size
props.description: The input's size.

props.name: filled
props.description: Draws a filled input.

props.name: pill
props.description: Draws a pill-style input with rounded edges.

props.name: label
props.description: The input's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

props.name: help-text
props.description: The input's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: clearable
props.description: Adds a clear button when the input is not empty.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the input.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the input is empty.

props.name: readonly
props.description: Makes the input readonly.

props.name: password-toggle
props.description: Adds a button to toggle the password's visibility. Only applies to password types.

props.name: password-visible
props.description: Determines whether or not the password is currently visible. Only applies to password input types.

props.name: no-spin-buttons
props.description: Hides the browser's built-in increment/decrement spin buttons for number inputs.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: required
props.description: Makes the input a required field.

props.name: pattern
props.description: A regular expression pattern to validate input against.

props.name: minlength
props.description: The minimum length of input that will be considered valid.

props.name: maxlength
props.description: The maximum length of input that will be considered valid.

props.name: min
props.description: The input's minimum value. Only applies to date and number input types.

props.name: max
props.description: The input's maximum value. Only applies to date and number input types.

props.name: step
props.description:
Specifies the granularity that the value must adhere to, or the special value `any` which means no stepping is
implied, allowing any numeric value. Only applies to date and number input types.

props.name: autocapitalize
props.description: Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered by the user.

props.name: autocorrect
props.description: Indicates whether the browser's autocorrect feature is on or off.

props.name: autocomplete
props.description:
Specifies what permission the browser has to provide assistance in filling out form field values. Refer to
[this page on MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete) for available values.

props.name: autofocus
props.description: Indicates that the input should receive focus on page load.

props.name: enterkeyhint
props.description: Used to customize the label or icon of the Enter key on virtual keyboards.

props.name: spellcheck
props.description: Enables spell checking on the input.

props.name: inputmode
props.description:
Tells the browser what type of data will be entered by the user, allowing it to display the appropriate virtual
keyboard on supportive devices.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: helpText
props.description: The input's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: passwordToggle
props.description: Adds a button to toggle the password's visibility. Only applies to password types.

props.name: passwordVisible
props.description: Determines whether or not the password is currently visible. Only applies to password input types.

props.name: noSpinButtons
props.description: Hides the browser's built-in increment/decrement spin buttons for number inputs.

props.name: valueAsDate
props.description: Gets or sets the current value as a `Date` object. Returns `null` if the value can't be converted. This will use the native `<input type="{{type}}">` implementation and may result in an error.

props.name: valueAsNumber
props.description: Gets or sets the current value as a number. Returns `NaN` if the value can't be converted.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the control loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onClear
props.description: Emitted when the clear button is activated. (CEM event "sl-clear")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the control gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the control receives input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: onSelect
props.description: Emitted when a menu item is selected. (CEM event "sl-select")

props.name: type
props.description: The type of menu item to render. To use `checked`, this value must be set to `checkbox`.

props.name: checked
props.description: Draws the item in a checked state.

props.name: value
props.description: A unique value to store in the menu item. This can be used as a way to identify menu items when selected.

props.name: loading
props.description: Draws the menu item in a loading state.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Draws the menu item in a disabled state, preventing selection.

props.name: attr
props.description:
Watches for changes to attributes. To watch only specific attributes, separate them by a space, e.g.
`attr="class id title"`. To watch all attributes, use `*`.

props.name: attr-old-value
props.description: Indicates whether or not the attribute's previous value should be recorded when monitoring changes.

props.name: char-data
props.description: Watches for changes to the character data contained within the node.

props.name: char-data-old-value
props.description: Indicates whether or not the previous value of the node's text should be recorded.

props.name: child-list
props.description: Watches for the addition or removal of new child nodes.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the observer.

props.name: attrOldValue
props.description: Indicates whether or not the attribute's previous value should be recorded when monitoring changes.

props.name: charData
props.description: Watches for changes to the character data contained within the node.

props.name: charDataOldValue
props.description: Indicates whether or not the previous value of the node's text should be recorded.

props.name: childList
props.description: Watches for the addition or removal of new child nodes.

props.name: onMutation
props.description: Emitted when a mutation occurs. (CEM event "sl-mutation")

props.name: value
props.description:
The option's value. When selected, the containing form control will receive this value. The value must be unique
from other options in the same group. Values may not contain spaces, as spaces are used as delimiters when listing
multiple values.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Draws the option in a disabled state, preventing selection.

props.name: anchor
props.description:
The element the popup will be anchored to. If the anchor lives outside of the popup, you can provide the anchor
element `id`, a DOM element reference, or a `VirtualElement`. If the anchor lives inside the popup, use the
`anchor` slot instead.

props.name: active
props.description:
Activates the positioning logic and shows the popup. When this attribute is removed, the positioning logic is torn
down and the popup will be hidden.

props.name: placement
props.description:
The preferred placement of the popup. Note that the actual placement will vary as configured to keep the
panel inside of the viewport.

props.name: strategy
props.description:
Determines how the popup is positioned. The `absolute` strategy works well in most cases, but if overflow is
clipped, using a `fixed` position strategy can often workaround it.

props.name: distance
props.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel away from its anchor.

props.name: skidding
props.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel along its anchor.

props.name: arrow
props.description:
Attaches an arrow to the popup. The arrow's size and color can be customized using the `--arrow-size` and
`--arrow-color` custom properties. For additional customizations, you can also target the arrow using
`::part(arrow)` in your stylesheet.

props.name: arrow-placement
props.description:
The placement of the arrow. The default is `anchor`, which will align the arrow as close to the center of the
anchor as possible, considering available space and `arrow-padding`. A value of `start`, `end`, or `center` will
align the arrow to the start, end, or center of the popover instead.

props.name: arrow-padding
props.description:
The amount of padding between the arrow and the edges of the popup. If the popup has a border-radius, for example,
this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

props.name: flip
props.description:
When set, placement of the popup will flip to the opposite site to keep it in view. You can use
`flipFallbackPlacements` to further configure how the fallback placement is determined.

props.name: flip-fallback-placements
props.description:
If the preferred placement doesn't fit, popup will be tested in these fallback placements until one fits. Must be a
string of any number of placements separated by a space, e.g. "top bottom left". If no placement fits, the flip
fallback strategy will be used instead.

props.name: flip-fallback-strategy
props.description:
When neither the preferred placement nor the fallback placements fit, this value will be used to determine whether
the popup should be positioned using the best available fit based on available space or as it was initially
preferred.

props.name: flipBoundary
props.description:
The flip boundary describes clipping element(s) that overflow will be checked relative to when flipping. By
default, the boundary includes overflow ancestors that will cause the element to be clipped. If needed, you can
change the boundary by passing a reference to one or more elements to this property.

props.name: flip-padding
props.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the flip behavior will occur.

props.name: shift
props.description: Moves the popup along the axis to keep it in view when clipped.

props.name: shiftBoundary
props.description:
The shift boundary describes clipping element(s) that overflow will be checked relative to when shifting. By
default, the boundary includes overflow ancestors that will cause the element to be clipped. If needed, you can
change the boundary by passing a reference to one or more elements to this property.

props.name: shift-padding
props.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the shift behavior will occur.

props.name: auto-size
props.description: When set, this will cause the popup to automatically resize itself to prevent it from overflowing.

props.name: sync
props.description: Syncs the popup's width or height to that of the anchor element.

props.name: autoSizeBoundary
props.description:
The auto-size boundary describes clipping element(s) that overflow will be checked relative to when resizing. By
default, the boundary includes overflow ancestors that will cause the element to be clipped. If needed, you can
change the boundary by passing a reference to one or more elements to this property.

props.name: auto-size-padding
props.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the auto-size behavior will occur.

props.name: hover-bridge
props.description:
When a gap exists between the anchor and the popup element, this option will add a "hover bridge" that fills the
gap using an invisible element. This makes listening for events such as `mouseenter` and `mouseleave` more sane
because the pointer never technically leaves the element. The hover bridge will only be drawn when the popover is
active.

props.name: popup
props.description: A reference to the internal popup container. Useful for animating and styling the popup with JavaScript.

props.name: arrowPlacement
props.description:
The placement of the arrow. The default is `anchor`, which will align the arrow as close to the center of the
anchor as possible, considering available space and `arrow-padding`. A value of `start`, `end`, or `center` will
align the arrow to the start, end, or center of the popover instead.

props.name: arrowPadding
props.description:
The amount of padding between the arrow and the edges of the popup. If the popup has a border-radius, for example,
this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

props.name: flipFallbackPlacements
props.description:
If the preferred placement doesn't fit, popup will be tested in these fallback placements until one fits. Must be a
string of any number of placements separated by a space, e.g. "top bottom left". If no placement fits, the flip
fallback strategy will be used instead.

props.name: flipFallbackStrategy
props.description:
When neither the preferred placement nor the fallback placements fit, this value will be used to determine whether
the popup should be positioned using the best available fit based on available space or as it was initially
preferred.

props.name: flipPadding
props.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the flip behavior will occur.

props.name: shiftPadding
props.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the shift behavior will occur.

props.name: autoSize
props.description: When set, this will cause the popup to automatically resize itself to prevent it from overflowing.

props.name: autoSizePadding
props.description: The amount of padding, in pixels, to exceed before the auto-size behavior will occur.

props.name: hoverBridge
props.description:
When a gap exists between the anchor and the popup element, this option will add a "hover bridge" that fills the
gap using an invisible element. This makes listening for events such as `mouseenter` and `mouseleave` more sane
because the pointer never technically leaves the element. The hover bridge will only be drawn when the popover is
active.

props.name: onReposition
props.description: Emitted when the popup is repositioned. This event can fire a lot, so avoid putting expensive operations in your listener or consider debouncing it. (CEM event "sl-reposition")

props.name: value
props.description: The current progress as a percentage, 0 to 100.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: When true, percentage is ignored, the label is hidden, and the progress bar is drawn in an indeterminate state.

props.name: label
props.description: A custom label for assistive devices.

props.name: value
props.description: The current progress as a percentage, 0 to 100.

props.name: label
props.description: A custom label for assistive devices.

props.name: value
props.description: The QR code's value.

props.name: label
props.description: The label for assistive devices to announce. If unspecified, the value will be used instead.

props.name: size
props.description: The size of the QR code, in pixels.

props.name: fill
props.description: The fill color. This can be any valid CSS color, but not a CSS custom property.

props.name: background
props.description: The background color. This can be any valid CSS color or `transparent`. It cannot be a CSS custom property.

props.name: radius
props.description: The edge radius of each module. Must be between 0 and 0.5.

props.name: error-correction
props.description: The level of error correction to use. [Learn more](https://www.qrcode.com/en/about/error_correction.html)

props.name: errorCorrection
props.description: The level of error correction to use. [Learn more](https://www.qrcode.com/en/about/error_correction.html)

props.name: value
props.description: The radio's value. When selected, the radio group will receive this value.

props.name: size
props.description:
The radio's size. When used inside a radio group, the size will be determined by the radio group's size so this
attribute can typically be omitted.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the radio.

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the control loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the control gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: value
props.description: The radio's value. When selected, the radio group will receive this value.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the radio button.

props.name: size
props.description:
The radio button's size. When used inside a radio group, the size will be determined by the radio group's size so
this attribute can typically be omitted.

props.name: pill
props.description: Draws a pill-style radio button with rounded edges.

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the button loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the button gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: label
props.description:
The radio group's label. Required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot
instead.

props.name: help-text
props.description: The radio groups's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: value
props.description: The current value of the radio group, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: size
props.description: The radio group's size. This size will be applied to all child radios and radio buttons.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: required
props.description: Ensures a child radio is checked before allowing the containing form to submit.

props.name: helpText
props.description: The radio groups's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when the radio group's selected value changes. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the radio group receives user input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the range, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: value
props.description: The current value of the range, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: label
props.description: The range's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

props.name: help-text
props.description: The range's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the help-text slot instead.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the range.

props.name: min
props.description: The minimum acceptable value of the range.

props.name: max
props.description: The maximum acceptable value of the range.

props.name: step
props.description: The interval at which the range will increase and decrease.

props.name: tooltip
props.description: The preferred placement of the range's tooltip.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: helpText
props.description: The range's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the help-text slot instead.

props.name: tooltipFormatter
props.description:
A function used to format the tooltip's value. The range's value is passed as the first and only argument. The
function should return a string to display in the tooltip.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the control loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the control gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the control receives input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: label
props.description: A label that describes the rating to assistive devices.

props.name: value
props.description: The current rating.

props.name: max
props.description: The highest rating to show.

props.name: precision
props.description:
The precision at which the rating will increase and decrease. For example, to allow half-star ratings, set this
attribute to `0.5`.

props.name: readonly
props.description: Makes the rating readonly.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the rating.

props.name: getSymbol
props.description:
A function that customizes the symbol to be rendered. The first and only argument is the rating's current value.
The function should return a string containing trusted HTML of the symbol to render at the specified value. Works
well with `<sl-icon>` elements.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when the rating's value changes. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onHover
props.description: Emitted when the user hovers over a value. The `phase` property indicates when hovering starts, moves to a new value, or ends. The `value` property tells what the rating's value would be if the user were to commit to the hovered value. (CEM event "sl-hover")

props.name: date
props.description:
The date from which to calculate time from. If not set, the current date and time will be used. When passing a
string, it's strongly recommended to use the ISO 8601 format to ensure timezones are handled correctly. To convert
a date to this format in JavaScript, use [`date.toISOString()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toISOString).

props.name: format
props.description: The formatting style to use.

props.name: numeric
props.description:
When `auto`, values such as "yesterday" and "tomorrow" will be shown when possible. When `always`, values such as
"1 day ago" and "in 1 day" will be shown.

props.name: sync
props.description: Keep the displayed value up to date as time passes.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the observer.

props.name: onResize
props.description: Emitted when the element is resized. (CEM event "sl-resize")

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the select, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: value
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: size
props.description: The select's size.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the select is empty.

props.name: multiple
props.description: Allows more than one option to be selected.

props.name: max-options-visible
props.description:
The maximum number of selected options to show when `multiple` is true. After the maximum, "+n" will be shown to
indicate the number of additional items that are selected. Set to 0 to remove the limit.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the select control.

props.name: clearable
props.description: Adds a clear button when the select is not empty.

props.name: open
props.description:
Indicates whether or not the select is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the menu, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the select's open state.

props.name: hoist
props.description:
Enable this option to prevent the listbox from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all, scenarios.

props.name: filled
props.description: Draws a filled select.

props.name: pill
props.description: Draws a pill-style select with rounded edges.

props.name: label
props.description: The select's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

props.name: placement
props.description:
The preferred placement of the select's menu. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the listbox
inside of the viewport.

props.name: help-text
props.description: The select's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: required
props.description: The select's required attribute.

props.name: getTag
props.description:
A function that customizes the tags to be rendered when multiple=true. The first argument is the option, the second
is the current tag's index.  The function should return either a Lit TemplateResult or a string containing trusted HTML of the symbol to render at
the specified value.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: maxOptionsVisible
props.description:
The maximum number of selected options to show when `multiple` is true. After the maximum, "+n" will be shown to
indicate the number of additional items that are selected. Set to 0 to remove the limit.

props.name: helpText
props.description: The select's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when the control's value changes. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onClear
props.description: Emitted when the control's value is cleared. (CEM event "sl-clear")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the control receives input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the control gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the control loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onShow
props.description: Emitted when the select's menu opens. (CEM event "sl-show")

props.name: onAfterShow
props.description: Emitted after the select's menu opens and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-show")

props.name: onHide
props.description: Emitted when the select's menu closes. (CEM event "sl-hide")

props.name: onAfterHide
props.description: Emitted after the select's menu closes and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-hide")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: effect
props.description: Determines which effect the skeleton will use.

props.name: position
props.description:
The current position of the divider from the primary panel's edge as a percentage 0-100. Defaults to 50% of the
container's initial size.

props.name: position-in-pixels
props.description: The current position of the divider from the primary panel's edge in pixels.

props.name: vertical
props.description: Draws the split panel in a vertical orientation with the start and end panels stacked.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables resizing. Note that the position may still change as a result of resizing the host element.

props.name: primary
props.description:
If no primary panel is designated, both panels will resize proportionally when the host element is resized. If a
primary panel is designated, it will maintain its size and the other panel will grow or shrink as needed when the
host element is resized.

props.name: snap
props.description:
Either one or more space-separated values at which the divider should snap, in pixels, percentages, or repeat expressions e.g. `'100px 50% 500px' or `repeat(50%) 10px`,
or a function which takes in a `SnapFunctionParams`, and returns a position to snap to, e.g. `({ pos }) => Math.round(pos / 8) * 8`.

props.name: snap-threshold
props.description: How close the divider must be to a snap point until snapping occurs.

props.name: positionInPixels
props.description: The current position of the divider from the primary panel's edge in pixels.

props.name: snapThreshold
props.description: How close the divider must be to a snap point until snapping occurs.

props.name: onReposition
props.description: Emitted when the divider's position changes. (CEM event "sl-reposition")

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: value
props.description: The current value of the switch, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: size
props.description: The switch's size.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the switch.

props.name: checked
props.description: Draws the switch in a checked state.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: required
props.description: Makes the switch a required field.

props.name: help-text
props.description: The switch's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: defaultChecked
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: helpText
props.description: The switch's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the control loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when the control's checked state changes. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the control receives input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the control gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: panel
props.description: The name of the tab panel this tab is associated with. The panel must be located in the same tab group.

props.name: active
props.description: Draws the tab in an active state.

props.name: closable
props.description: Makes the tab closable and shows a close button.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the tab and prevents selection.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Emitted when the tab is closable and the close button is activated. (CEM event "sl-close")

props.name: placement
props.description: The placement of the tabs.

props.name: activation
props.description:
When set to auto, navigating tabs with the arrow keys will instantly show the corresponding tab panel. When set to
manual, the tab will receive focus but will not show until the user presses spacebar or enter.

props.name: no-scroll-controls
props.description: Disables the scroll arrows that appear when tabs overflow.

props.name: fixed-scroll-controls
props.description: Prevent scroll buttons from being hidden when inactive.

props.name: noScrollControls
props.description: Disables the scroll arrows that appear when tabs overflow.

props.name: fixedScrollControls
props.description: Prevent scroll buttons from being hidden when inactive.

props.name: onTabShow
props.description: Emitted when a tab is shown. (CEM event "sl-tab-show")

props.name: onTabHide
props.description: Emitted when a tab is hidden. (CEM event "sl-tab-hide")

props.name: name
props.description: The tab panel's name.

props.name: active
props.description: When true, the tab panel will be shown.

props.name: variant
props.description: The tag's theme variant.

props.name: size
props.description: The tag's size.

props.name: pill
props.description: Draws a pill-style tag with rounded edges.

props.name: removable
props.description: Makes the tag removable and shows a remove button.

props.name: onRemove
props.description: Emitted when the remove button is activated. (CEM event "sl-remove")

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the textarea, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: value
props.description: The current value of the textarea, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.

props.name: size
props.description: The textarea's size.

props.name: filled
props.description: Draws a filled textarea.

props.name: label
props.description: The textarea's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

props.name: help-text
props.description: The textarea's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the input is empty.

props.name: rows
props.description: The number of rows to display by default.

props.name: resize
props.description: Controls how the textarea can be resized.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the textarea.

props.name: readonly
props.description: Makes the textarea readonly.

props.name: form
props.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.

props.name: required
props.description: Makes the textarea a required field.

props.name: minlength
props.description: The minimum length of input that will be considered valid.

props.name: maxlength
props.description: The maximum length of input that will be considered valid.

props.name: autocapitalize
props.description: Controls whether and how text input is automatically capitalized as it is entered by the user.

props.name: autocorrect
props.description: Indicates whether the browser's autocorrect feature is on or off.

props.name: autocomplete
props.description:
Specifies what permission the browser has to provide assistance in filling out form field values. Refer to
[this page on MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete) for available values.

props.name: autofocus
props.description: Indicates that the input should receive focus on page load.

props.name: enterkeyhint
props.description: Used to customize the label or icon of the Enter key on virtual keyboards.

props.name: spellcheck
props.description: Enables spell checking on the textarea.

props.name: inputmode
props.description:
Tells the browser what type of data will be entered by the user, allowing it to display the appropriate virtual
keyboard on supportive devices.

props.name: helpText
props.description: The textarea's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.

props.name: validity
props.description: Gets the validity state object

props.name: validationMessage
props.description: Gets the validation message

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Emitted when the control loses focus. (CEM event "sl-blur")

props.name: onChange
props.description: Emitted when an alteration to the control's value is committed by the user. (CEM event "sl-change")

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Emitted when the control gains focus. (CEM event "sl-focus")

props.name: onInput
props.description: Emitted when the control receives input. (CEM event "sl-input")

props.name: onInvalid
props.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied. (CEM event "sl-invalid")

props.name: content
props.description: The tooltip's content. If you need to display HTML, use the `content` slot instead.

props.name: placement
props.description:
The preferred placement of the tooltip. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the tooltip
inside of the viewport.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the tooltip so it won't show when triggered.

props.name: distance
props.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the tooltip away from its target.

props.name: open
props.description: Indicates whether or not the tooltip is open. You can use this in lieu of the show/hide methods.

props.name: skidding
props.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the tooltip along its target.

props.name: trigger
props.description:
Controls how the tooltip is activated. Possible options include `click`, `hover`, `focus`, and `manual`. Multiple
options can be passed by separating them with a space. When manual is used, the tooltip must be activated
programmatically.

props.name: hoist
props.description:
Enable this option to prevent the tooltip from being clipped when the component is placed inside a container with
`overflow: auto|hidden|scroll`. Hoisting uses a fixed positioning strategy that works in many, but not all,
scenarios.

props.name: onShow
props.description: Emitted when the tooltip begins to show. (CEM event "sl-show")

props.name: onAfterShow
props.description: Emitted after the tooltip has shown and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-show")

props.name: onHide
props.description: Emitted when the tooltip begins to hide. (CEM event "sl-hide")

props.name: onAfterHide
props.description: Emitted after the tooltip has hidden and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-hide")

props.name: selection
props.description:
The selection behavior of the tree. Single selection allows only one node to be selected at a time. Multiple
displays checkboxes and allows more than one node to be selected. Leaf allows only leaf nodes to be selected.

props.name: onSelectionChange
props.description: Emitted when a tree item is selected or deselected. (CEM event "sl-selection-change")

props.name: expanded
props.description: Expands the tree item.

props.name: selected
props.description: Draws the tree item in a selected state.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the tree item.

props.name: lazy
props.description: Enables lazy loading behavior.

props.name: onExpand
props.description: Emitted when the tree item expands. (CEM event "sl-expand")

props.name: onAfterExpand
props.description: Emitted after the tree item expands and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-expand")

props.name: onCollapse
props.description: Emitted when the tree item collapses. (CEM event "sl-collapse")

props.name: onAfterCollapse
props.description: Emitted after the tree item collapses and all animations are complete. (CEM event "sl-after-collapse")

props.name: onLazyChange
props.description: Emitted when the tree item's lazy state changes. (CEM event "sl-lazy-change")

props.name: onLazyLoad
props.description: Emitted when a lazy item is selected. Use this event to asynchronously load data and append items to the tree before expanding. After appending new items, remove the `lazy` attribute to remove the loading state and update the tree. (CEM event "sl-lazy-load")
